summary: Matched the ftCommon rebirth animation callback ftCo_Rebirth_Anim. The stub marker in src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c was replaced with a small implementation that reads Fighter from gobj->user_data, calls ftCo_8008A7A8 with fp->ft_data->x24, decrements fp->mv.co.common.x0, and transitions via ftCo_800D5600 when the counter reaches zero. The header prototype for ftCo_800D5600 was also upgraded from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj). PR body reports a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0.
what_changed: Replaced the /// #ftCo_Rebirth_Anim placeholder with a matching C implementation.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Anim(Fighter_GObj* gobj) using Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data.
Called ftCo_8008A7A8(gobj, fp->ft_data->x24) during the rebirth animation callback.
Decremented the common movement counter fp->mv.co.common.x0 and called ftCo_800D5600(gobj) when it reached zero.
Refined the declaration of ftCo_800D5600 from UNK_RET ftCo_800D5600(UNK_PARAMS) to void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
smart_moves: Kept the matched animation callback minimal and state-machine oriented, avoiding unnecessary temporaries beyond the Fighter* local visible in the diff.
Used existing fighter object access patterns: Fighter_GObj* input, gobj->user_data to Fighter*, and nested ft_data/common movement fields.
Resolved a neighboring unknown prototype only as far as required by the new callsite, improving type information without broad unrelated header churn.
Validated the match with both a clean ninja build and reported fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0.
decomp_lessons: Small fighter animation callbacks may be driven by a countdown stored under fp->mv.co.common.x0; when the counter reaches zero they transition to the next common-state helper.
When decompiling a callback, inspect its outgoing calls: matching the callback can justify replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with exact Fighter_GObj* signatures.
Use ft_data fields directly when the generated code needs to pass fighter-specific data into common helper routines; here fp->ft_data->x24 was the argument to ftCo_8008A7A8.
For ftCommon state callbacks, preserving the canonical signatures void func(Fighter_GObj* gobj) is important for header consistency and call matching.
naming_conventions: Common fighter-state callbacks use the ftCo_ prefix and state/action suffixes such as Rebirth_Anim, Rebirth_IASA, Rebirth_Phys, and Rebirth_Coll.
Unidentified address-based helper names such as ftCo_800D5600 remain until behavior is understood, but their prototypes should still be typed when evidence permits.
Fighter object parameters are named gobj, and the Fighter* extracted from gobj->user_data is named fp.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched by expressing the state timer as an in-place decrement of fp->mv.co.common.x0 followed by an equality check against zero.
Used the exact typed call ftCo_800D5600(gobj), which required replacing the previous UNK_PARAMS prototype.
Relied on the standard Fighter_GObj to Fighter user_data load pattern to line up the field accesses.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states: Decompile ftCo_Rebirth_Anim — 100% match.
The PR body states verification with ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document ftCo_800D5600.
Search for other ftCommon callbacks that decrement fp->mv.co.common.x0 before transitioning.
Inspect ftCo_8008A7A8 callsites to clarify the meaning of fp->ft_data->x24.
Compare Rebirth_Anim and RebirthWait_Anim implementations for shared state-machine patterns.
